root_text.sn35.43:1.5: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ, rūpā aniccā, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ, cakkhusamphasso anicco.
root_text.sn35.43:1.6: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.43:1.7: jivhā aniccā, rasā aniccā, jivhāviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ, jivhāsamphasso anicco.
root_text.sn35.43:1.8: Yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ.
root_text.sn35.43:1.10: mano anicco, dhammā aniccā, manoviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ, manosamphasso anicco.
root_text.sn35.43:1.11: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ.
root_text.sn35.43:1.12: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.43:1.13: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.43:1.14: manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati, manosamphassepi nibbindati,
root_text.sn35.43:1.15: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.43:1.16: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.43:1.17: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.43:1.4: And what is the all that is impermanent?
translation_text.sn35.43:1.5: The eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.43:1.6: And the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.43:1.10: The mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact are impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.43:1.11: The painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.43:1.12: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned …
translation_text.sn35.43:1.17: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
translation_text.sn35.46:1.1: “Mendicants, all is to be directly known. …”
translation_text.sn35.47:1.1: “Mendicants, all is to be completely understood. …”
translation_text.sn35.48:1.1: “Mendicants, all is to be given up. …”
translation_text.sn35.50:1.1: “Mendicants, all is to be directly known and completely understood. …”
